seoTitle: How to Create a Client Portal in ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to build a branded client portal in ClickUp with a welcome hub, progress dashboard, feedback form, and secure guest permissions.
intro: Sharing project progress with clients often means giving them just enough visibility without exposing your internal workspace. This guide covers building a dedicated client portal in ClickUp using Docs for a welcome hub, Dashboards for progress tracking, and restricted Guest permissions so clients only see what's meant for them.

steps.heading: Set up a Client Portal Space or Folder
steps.body: Create a dedicated area in your workspace hierarchy specifically for client-facing content.

steps.heading: Design a welcome hub with ClickUp Docs
steps.body: Build a central document with contract details, project scope, and links to key stakeholder resources.

steps.heading: Create a shared progress dashboard
steps.body: Add a Dashboard with visual task lists, progress bars, and a Gantt roadmap so clients can see status at a glance.

steps.heading: Add a feedback intake form
steps.body: Use a ClickUp Form view to give clients a way to submit feedback, asset links, or change requests directly into your workflow.

steps.heading: Set granular Guest permissions
steps.body: Use the sharing engine to invite clients as restricted Guests with View-Only or Comment-only access.

steps.heading: Test the portal from a guest's perspective
steps.body: Log in with a guest profile link to confirm sensitive internal data, chats, and budgets stay hidden from the client view.

faq.question: Can clients edit tasks inside the portal?
faq.answer: That depends on the permission level you set, since Guests can be restricted to View-Only or Comment access rather than full editing rights.

faq.question: Will clients see my internal team chats?
faq.answer: No, if you configure permissions correctly, internal workspace data, chats, and budgets remain hidden from Guest accounts.

faq.question: Can clients submit their own feedback through the portal?
faq.answer: Yes, a ClickUp Form view lets clients submit feedback, asset links, or change requests directly into your production pipeline.

faq.question: How do I check that my client can't see private data before sharing the link?
faq.answer: Test the portal using a guest profile link yourself to verify the experience matches what you intended before sending it to a real client.
